stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

To prepare for the marathon, Daniel trained four days a week, kept a strict diet, and ______
explanation: The list is past-tense verb phrases ('trained,' 'kept'). The third item has to match: 'slept eight hours each night.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

The professor advised her students not only to attend every lecture but also ______

choices.text: that they should take detailed notes.
they needed to take detailed notes.

explanation: 'Not only X but also Y' needs parallel forms. 'To attend' is an infinitive, so 'to take detailed notes' must also be an infinitive.

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

She enjoys hiking in the mountains, swimming in the lake, and ______

choices.text: to bike along the river trail.
she bikes along the river trail.

explanation: The list is gerunds ('hiking,' 'swimming'). Match with 'biking.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

The coach praised the team for their effort, their communication, and ______

choices.text: that they worked as a team.

explanation: The list is noun phrases with 'their' ('their effort,' 'their communication'). Match with 'their teamwork.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

The new policy will increase funding for libraries, reduce class sizes, and ______
explanation: The list is base-verb phrases ('increase,' 'reduce'). Match: 'improve teacher training.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Writing in the morning is more productive for me than ______
explanation: Compare a gerund ('writing in the morning') with a gerund ('writing at night').

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

My grandmother was both a talented musician and ______
explanation: 'Both X and Y' must be parallel. 'A talented musician' is a noun phrase; match with 'a dedicated teacher.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

The architect's new building is striking for its bold lines, its dramatic lighting, and ______
explanation: The list is 'its X, its Y, and its Z.' Match the pattern.

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Neither the cost of the repairs nor ______ discouraged the homeowners from restoring the old barn.
explanation: 'Neither X nor Y' needs parallel forms. 'The cost of the repairs' is a noun phrase; match with 'the difficulty of the work.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

A good presentation requires preparing your material carefully, practicing your delivery, and ______

choices.text: that you engage with your audience.

explanation: The list is gerunds. Match 'engaging.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

The vacation was relaxing not only because of the beautiful scenery but also ______

choices.text: of how friendly the people were.

explanation: 'Not only because of X but also because of Y.' Match: 'because of the friendly people.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

The job requires strong writing skills, attention to detail, and ______
explanation: The list is noun phrases. Match 'the ability to work independently.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

He preferred to read rather than ______
explanation: 'Rather than' with 'to read' (infinitive) needs an infinitive on the other side: 'to watch television.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

The new employee is hardworking, detail-oriented, and ______
explanation: The list is adjectives ('hardworking,' 'detail-oriented'). Match: adjective 'enthusiastic.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Either the principal or ______ will attend the parent meeting.

choices.text: they are going to send a teacher

explanation: 'Either X or Y' must be parallel. 'The principal' is a noun phrase; match with 'one of the teachers.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

The students learned how to formulate a hypothesis, how to design an experiment, and ______

choices.text: that they should analyze the results.

explanation: The list is 'how to X' phrases. Match: 'how to analyze the results.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

The chef is known for using local ingredients, creating unusual flavor combinations, and ______
explanation: The list is gerund phrases ('using,' 'creating'). Match: 'presenting.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

The tour guide spoke about the history of the building, explained its architectural features, and ______
explanation: The list is past-tense verb phrases. Match: 'answered visitors' questions.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

The author's novels are known for ______ than for their intricate plots.

choices.text: the vividness of their characters more

explanation: 'For X more than for Y' requires parallel 'for' phrases. 'For their vivid characters more... than for their intricate plots.'

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

The workshop covered how to write a résumé, how to prepare for interviews, and ______

choices.text: that you should negotiate a salary.

explanation: The list is 'how to X' phrases. Match: 'how to negotiate a salary.'
